summary: The daemon has three execution centers whose traits encode the order of flow: Signal (communication) processes the incom
nota: (Supersede ([g3f2 o8x5] [([(Technology (Software (Distributed EventDrivenArchitecture)))] Decision [The daemon has three execution centers whose traits encode the order of flow: Signal (communication) processes the incoming signal and pushes to Nexus; Nexus is the mail keeper and translator (mail held by Nexus is in the being-processed state) that gates the signal and translates it to a SEMA query; SEMA (state) runs and produces a state change plus a SEMA reply; then the flow reverses — Nexus translates the SEMA reply (carrying the durable-receipt marker) back into a Signal response and Signal emits it. Push happens only on success: an invalid message is rejected to the client directly; a valid signal proceeds to Nexus, and only an accepted signal proceeds to SEMA. Push methods take the next engine as a parameter so trait signatures themselves sequence Signal then Nexus then SEMA then back. UI and external-IO uses of Nexus are specific uses of its fundamental in-between translator and mail-keeper role.] Medium VeryHigh Zero [signal-engine nexus-engine sema-engine daemon signal-frame])] ([([I want its size reduced, negative guidelines removed unless absolutely necessary, and many records agglomerated if they obviously can be worded in a unified record instead of many] (Some [psyche directive: deep audit of the spirit database]))] [Agglomeration: Both records describe the identical Signal->Nexus->SEMA->reversal flow with trait-encoded ordering and push-only-on-success. o8x5 names the three execution centers and the mail-keeper/translator role; g3f2 names the three engines and parameter-passing trait sequencing. summary: Intent capture is an argued case judged by a guardian, modeled on a court of law: the submitting agent is an advocate; t
nota: (Supersede ([ll41 z3ka ans1 7xnx so3b woku bwqe] [([(Technology (Software (Distributed ProtocolDesign)))] Decision [Intent capture is an argued case judged by a guardian, modeled on a court of law: the submitting agent is an advocate; the justification is the strongly-typed argued case (never a stringly blob) carrying typed verbatim psyche testimony (spans surrounded by asterisks) with surrounding context, optional antecedents, and reasoning for why the record and its certainty, domain, kind, and importance are right. The claim is the operation's own Entry, not a justification sub-field; redundant citation-signal tags are excluded, with a cited record relationship carried by the operation or by reasoning prose. The guardian renders one binary yes/no across those dimensions, judging any cross-record operation (e.g. replacing several records with several new ones) as one whole. Certainty is the burden of proof the verbatim quotes must clear on their modal strength: over-claim on hedged words is rejected, the guardian never silently lowers it nor rewrites could into should, so honest understatement is rational and tentative wording stays low and unrewritten; a rejection sends the rewording back to the highest-thinking-power submitting agent. summary: Forge is the workspace's emerging build-system component family, the eventual Nix replacement. forge-core is the standar
nota: (Supersede ([gopu hfbr] [([(Technology (Software (Operations BuildSystem)))] Decision [Forge is the workspace's emerging build-system component family, the eventual Nix replacement. forge-core is the standardisation contract sub-component forges adopt as the ecosystem matures; each sema-upgrade-shaped component may carry its own forge with its own language until it standardises on forge-core. summary: In a schema a PascalCase (capital-first) name declares or references a TYPE; a camelCase (lowercase-first) name binds a
nota: (Supersede ([5jac 4idp 4fwy mchq 7oqk] [([(Technology (Software (Programming TypeSystems)))] Decision [In a schema a PascalCase (capital-first) name declares or references a TYPE; a camelCase (lowercase-first) name binds a FIELD for code access. There is no explicit field-name syntax: a field name derives by lowercasing its PascalCase type, so a declaration is a bare list of type names (Entry (Topic Kind Summary Context Certainty Quote)) — reversing operator/180's added SchemaField name+type. A PascalCase type may be declared inline at first use and reused by name; resolution is declare-before-use, left to right, enabling single-pass parsing. A type becomes library/export visible by being a top-level namespace entry; the namespace is conceptually the struct of declared types, while enums are category divisions with payload carried by variants. A field name divergent from its type comes from a newtype giving the type its own name ((Certainty (Magnitude)) used as (Certainty)) — no per-position override. NOTA records carry no field names; schema supplies meaning through named type boundaries.] High Minimum Zero [schema nota schema-next newtype PascalCase camelCase])] ([([I want its size reduced, negative guidelines removed unless absolutely necessary, and many records agglomerated if they obviously can be worded in a unified record instead of many] (Some [psyche directive: deep audit of the spirit database]))] [Agglomeration: All five state one coherent rule: PascalCase=type / camelCase=field, field names derived by lowercasing PascalCase type names (no explicit field syntax), divergent names via newtype, inline reusable declarations, export via top-level namespace. Restored two arrows the first pass dropped: 4fwy's reversal of operator/180's SchemaField, and 4idp's namespace-is-struct / enum-is-category-division. mchq's no-per-position-override preserved.])))

summary: criome's identity-policy clock is decentralized quorum-attested coarse crystallized-PAST time, not a trusted or precise
nota: (ChangeRecord (ay3y ([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture))) (Technology (Software (Distributed ProtocolDesign)))] Decision [criome's identity-policy clock is decentralized quorum-attested coarse crystallized-PAST time, not a trusted or precise wall-clock. A time attestation is a forward-extending window, content-addressed by its proposition; a quorum signs that the present falls within it, and it closes at last signature or expiry, becoming a non-forgeable monotonic lower bound on now. Only the past is provable. summary: criome is a universal authorization-agreement machine. On admitting a content-addressed object/state transition it emits
nota: (ChangeRecord (m0p2 ([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture))) (Technology (Software (Distributed ProtocolDesign))) (Technology (Software (Security Authentication)))] Decision [criome is a universal authorization-agreement machine. On admitting a content-addressed object/state transition it emits an authorized-object reference (not the payload); the router, sole matcher for non-direct messages, fans the reference to subscribed components, which fetch the rkyv object via the routing/object-distribution layer. Meaningful objects need quorum authorization, not lone-node signatures. Time-driven pulses are programmed per-contract: an after-time acceptance schedules a later check that, on maturity, re-checks related events and triggers a new quorum-signed acceptance — no ambient heartbeat. criome keeps no operational delivery registry; any local subscription surface is observation/audit only, with the direct criome-to-criome lane the sole non-router path.] Medium Minimum Zero [criome router rkyv]) ([([I want its size reduced, negative guidelines removed unless absolutely necessary, and many records agglomerated if they obviously can be worded in a unified record instead of many] (Some [psyche directive: deep audit of the spirit database]))] [Condense mini-essay to terse arrow: Condensed restatement-heavy record to operative arrows: reference-emit + router fan-out, quorum default, programmed time-pulse, no delivery registry. nota: (ChangeRecord (lt44 ([(Technology (Software (Engineering Architecture)))] Decision [CriomOS has two transport lanes. Router is the ordinary fabric for cross-sandbox and cross-network component/object traffic (router-to-router routed delivery, reference push, payload-blind byte move/fetch beyond routing/auth/delivery policy). criome has a direct criome-to-criome peer lane for time-sensitive agreement (quorum signing, crystallized-time windows) carrying only agreement/authorization messages, so criome stays auth/agreement-only and router stays general transport. summary: Everything is a struct: a unit variant is a zero-field struct, a data-carrying variant a one-field struct, an enum a sin
nota: (ChangeRecord (umsv ([(Technology (Software (Programming DomainSpecificLanguages)))] Decision [Everything is a struct: a unit variant is a zero-field struct, a data-carrying variant a one-field struct, an enum a single-field struct holding the active variant. A schema node is a tagged variant — a paren whose first object is the tag and second is the carried data — read against the KNOWN type expected at its position, so the tag picks the variant with no sigil. A macro is a Rust struct defining its arguments; the macro-space is a tagged union of those structs; invoking a macro is a tagged variant read against the known macro/reference type, again sigil-free. Positional typing (every position read against the schema-of-schemas) dissolves the enum-versus-macro ambiguity. Supersedes the at-sigil macro invocation of record 1087.] High High Zero [schema macro nota rust umsv]) ([([I want its size reduced, negative guidelines removed unless absolutely necessary, and many records agglomerated if they obviously can be worded in a unified record instead of many] (Some [psyche directive: deep audit of the spirit database]))] [Condense mini-essay to terse arrow: Mini-essay with restatement and an implementation-focus tail ('represent the assembled schema... first so we know how to compose a macro' — task state). Condensed to the operative model: everything-is-struct, sigil-free positional typing, macro-as-struct, supersession of 1087. Importance stays High.])))

note: One long run-on with repeated restatement of 'position determines type'. Condensed to the operative recursive model, removing repetition while keeping every structural rule.
summary: The schema language is one recursive shape: a root struct (the dialect-level Schema type) with positional fields of know
nota: (ChangeRecord (wt7s ([(Technology (Software (Programming DomainSpecificLanguages)))] Decision [The schema language is one recursive shape: a root struct (the dialect-level Schema type) with positional fields of known types, macro-expanded down to scalar leaves. Vector fields carry variable-count macro-call instances; macros are structural expansion by position; inner struct/enum declarations are macros firing at their positions; objects inside a macro call match its pattern; enum bodies re-parse because the enum name carries forward. Recursion terminates at built-in macros expanding leaf structs/enums of scalar types (vectors of integers, vectors of unit-variant enums, booleans, integers, strings). Strings are typed newtypes around character sequences for identity. Position determines type, macros do structural expansion, leaves are scalars.] Medium Medium Zero [schema macro Schema struct enum]) ([([I want its size reduced, negative guidelines removed unless absolutely necessary, and many records agglomerated if they obviously can be worded in a unified record instead of many] (Some [psyche directive: deep audit of the spirit database]))] [Condense mini-essay to terse arrow: One long run-on with repeated restatement of 'position determines type'. Condensed to the operative recursive model, removing repetition while keeping every structural rule.])))

summary: Pipe-brace traits and impls are data: a catalog referencing impls and methods already existing on the Rust side so schem
nota: (ChangeRecord (ba6d ([(Technology (Software (Programming TypeSystems))) (Technology (Software (Programming Grammars)))] Decision [Pipe-brace traits and impls are data: a catalog referencing impls and methods already existing on the Rust side so schema can name them and method-calling syntax can invoke them from schema-authored bodies. An impl fuses onto its target type as one combined object — a type declaration carries a trailing pipe-brace impl block with the target implicit, introducing a type and its trait participations and method signatures together with no separate marker objects. Each impl-block entry is a bare trait atom (marker impl), a trait atom followed by a bracket of method signatures (body-bearing trait impl), or a bare method signature (inherent method); a method signature is name then a brace parameter struct then a return type, the same shape as a Work-frame leg, naming a referenced existing impl rather than a generated body. Method calls resolve from receiver schema shape plus this fused impl catalog. Generating bodies from schema stays reserved for the narrow mechanical family; deref-by-default is not a goal (it was only scaffolding for impl syntax).] High Minimum Zero [nota nota-next schema-next schema-cc impl trait PipeBrace Rust]) ([([I want its size reduced, negative guidelines removed unless absolutely necessary, and many records agglomerated if they obviously can be worded in a unified record instead of many] (Some [psyche directive: deep audit of the spirit database]))] [Condense mini-essay to terse arrow: Strips the one rhetorical aside ('because separate objects add too much association complexity') while keeping every operative arrow.
